description: Create a content slide on which transformation initiatives will require the most CRO attention over the next five years, using a sidebar quote on the left and horizontal bar charts with explanatory text in the main area.

header.kicker: SECOND ANNUAL EY/FT GLOBAL INSURANCE RISK MANAGEMENT SURVEY
header.headline: Of your organization's planned transformation and growth initiatives, which will require the most CRO attention in the next five years?

sidebar.title: Survey respondent
sidebar.body: EY-Parthenon CEO Outlook Survey shows that CEOs would certainly welcome more CRO engagement with change initiatives. When asked where their organization needs to improve their approach to transformation, 68% of CEOs say more collaboration across functions and the C-suite was key, their top answer.

main.body:
To be clear, CROs are investing most of their time in traditional risk management activities: 83% of CROs spend at least 25% of their time on core tasks. But significant effort is also going into advising the business (49% of CROs spend a quarter or more of their time in this way), enterprise-wide change initiatives (42%) and the business strategic planning process (31%). In contrast, only 24% of CROs spend as much time on remediation activities. This is strong evidence of the value of taking a proactive approach to risk management.

Looking out five years, more than half of CROs (54% overall) say they expect sustainability-related and climate-related change initiatives to demand most of their time and attention, followed by adoption of GenAI, which was cited by 38% of respondents. Interestingly, GenAI was the top initiative cited in last year's survey.

Sixty-eight percent of CROs at property and casualty (P&C) carriers are focused on climate and environmental initiatives, reflecting the greater exposure in that line of business. The findings also make clear that CROs are taking a long-term view of environmental and climate risks, with regional variations largely a factor of regulatory priorities across jurisdictions.

Chart data:
- Environmental, social and governance (ESG)/climate risk management: 54% (74% EMEIA | 32% Americas | 38% Asia-Pacific)
- Use of GenAI: 38% (30% EMEIA | 43% Americas | 54% Asia-Pacific)
- Mergers and acquisitions: 27% (30% EMEIA | 14% Americas | 46% Asia-Pacific)
- Product diversification and/or market expansion: 24% (19% EMEIA | 25% Americas | 31% Asia-Pacific)
- Innovation in product development: 21% (12% EMEIA | 29% Americas | 31% Asia-Pacific)
- Strategic partnerships and alliances: 21% (21% EMEIA | 29% Americas | 18% Asia-Pacific)
- Modernizing core functions and platforms: 16% (21% EMEIA | 14% Americas | 18% Asia-Pacific)
